evals.name: email-boundary-test
evals.prompt: I need to test a Python login flow that sends email. Should I mock the mailer, use a fake, or write an integration test?
evals.expected_output: Names the boundary, chooses fake/mock/real dependency deliberately, keeps behavior assertions primary, and preserves boundary coverage.

evals.name: mock-heavy-test-refactor
evals.prompt: This Python unit test has six patches and only asserts calls. How should I rescue it?
evals.expected_output: Flags mock smell, distinguishes behavior from implementation, suggests fake or higher boundary, and records what real integration proof remains needed.
